title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot discusses the murder of Joyce Reynolds with her family physician. The doctor explains that Joyce was generally healthy, suffering only occasionally from biliary disease, and had no unusual medical history. When Poirot suggests that Joyce may have been killed because she "said too much," the doctor notes that while witness testimony can provide a motive, the killer might simply be driven by a disturbed or evil mind. The doctor posits that the murderer was likely present at the Halloween party, potentially acting as a guest, a servant, or an intruder who entered through a window after checking the latches.

Poirot then visits the Elm Tree School to meet Miss Emery. He explains that he is investigating the murder at the invitation of Mrs. Ariadne Oliver. Miss Emery describes Joyce as an ordinary child who had a compulsive habit of lying to attract attention and boast to her friends. She suggests that Joyce’s claim of witnessing a murder earlier that day was likely an exaggeration of a mundane event, such as a car accident, intended to impress others or Mrs. Oliver. Miss Emery notes that most of the children at the party were between nine and fifteen years old and were associated with the school. She also mentions a previous unsolved crime at the school: the strangulation of a melancholy twenty-four-year-old teacher named Miss White, which occurred a year or two prior.
